content:
# Dykstra Parsons Coefficient

**Summary**: A measure of the heterogeneity of permeability in a reservoir.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021]] calculated Dykstra-Parsons coefficients to quantify permeability heterogeneity.

## Scientific Context

- [[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021]] Used to assess the degree of flow path variability.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021]] calculated from porosity and permeability data.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Dykstra-Parsons coefficient ranged from 0.650 to 0.990. ([[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021, p. 13]])

## Contrasting Values and Interpretations

- [[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021]] 0.650–0.990
